title: Body posture
wikitext:
[]: Body posture             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 16, 2008 4:27 pm I've noticed something: if you look at the body posture of the 3 Eva pilots throughout the series, each of them consistently has their own distinct body posture: Asuka is always "confident"; feet wide, hands on hips Shinji is always limp and neutral; hands laying limply at his sides like any other character Rei hangs on herself; you know, when a child hangs one arm on another arm, or she intertwines her legs around each other. ****what I think is odd, is that Shinji and Rei's body postures seem to have been interchanged If *Rei* is the one who is "utterly removed from her surroundings", and ***displays no facial expressions or emotions*....why DOES she display emotion through body posture? Because it seems to ME, that as Shinji is the "outwardly nervous one"....HE should be the one who has an "outwardly nervous body posture", and Rei should be the one whose body posture is "limp and emotionless"

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Aug 16, 2008 4:32 pm OVERANALYZING.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Aug 16, 2008 4:39 pm I remember noting this (well, body language in general, not what you went into) a long while back and nobody seemed to care so I left it alone. Playing poker like I do I've become a bit of a student at studying body language (even though I play mostly online I do make it to casinos now and then), and you're pretty much right. Asuka consistently assumes a "power stance". Shinji is almost always slouched, even if it's to a slight degree. Rei I've never really noticed but it seems you're right. Next time I watch NGE I'll pay more attention to this because it's something I only kinda picked up on intuitively previously.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Aug 16, 2008 4:50 pm AuraTwilight wrote: OVERANALYZING.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Aug 16, 2008 5:14 pm It really isn't overanalysing. A character's posture gives us loads of information at a glance and is something the director would have to consider. It would be overanalysing if V had said "hey, Rei's posture where her hand crosses in front of her body is really similar to the rajayami position in satriya symbolic dance, this is relevant because of x, y and z - we can also draw some parallels to 1 of the 400 postures used in noh" - or something along those lines.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Sat Aug 16, 2008 5:26 pm Action_Bastard wrote: AuraTwilight wrote: OVERANALYZING.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sat Aug 16, 2008 5:59 pm Besides, overanalysis is what this forum is about.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 16, 2008 7:34 pm it is incongruent to complaint that "we don't discuss Eva anymore and the forums are slow" and at the same time constantly declare that things brought up into discussion are either "Thread Necromancy" or "Over-analysis" I mean I'd support someone analyzing the entire series for damaged/smudges frames that have the "Tree of Life" in them.....so long as it wasn't "fanwanking", but self-admittedly "I just like to have a list of all the errors"; hard facts. but I digress...... ****something about Rei's posture: my point is I think it weird that she "hangs on herself" in several shots when arguably she should be a limp doll or "blank" ....isn't there something about autistic people that its common for them to like, bang their head on a desk rhythmically or OCD touch and rub stuff/wring their hands? I've heard some later describe it (in reports, not personally) as "its like reminding myself that my head is there"....sort of like, something in the brain that normally deals with fine motor coordination and (what's the word for "internal spacial sense"? I know there's a word for that) are not connected well, so rhythmic tapping or simply "obsessive compulsive fidgeting" is a common autistic thing? Or, is this just a matter of the background artists screwing up? I don't notice it in EVERY shot; there are many shots where Rei IS indeed limp, but there are also MULTIPLE shots where she's "fidgeting"; i.e. the end of episode 12 she's "hanging on herself" this might just be an animation goof ***Of course, the ultimate reason why I am concerned with the body posture of the main characters is not purely knowledge-for-knowledge's sake, but because if/when Live Action Eva is created this would be a good tip for the live-action actors to know in order to give a more accurate method-acting portrayal. Have Faith.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sat Aug 16, 2008 7:45 pm V wrote: ****something about Rei's posture: my point is I think it weird that she "hangs on herself" in several shots when arguably she should be a limp doll or "blank"

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 16, 2008 8:44 pm I will look for stuff like this when I watch the entire show, the director's cuts, and the movie tomorrow. Yay stuck in a car for about 8 hours! i get to go with my sister back to college in Ohio to unload her stuff =/

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 16, 2008 8:54 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: I will look for stuff like this when I watch the entire show, the director's cuts, and the movie tomorrow. Yay stuck in a car for about 8 hours! i get to go with my sister back to college in Ohio to unload her stuff =/

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:22 pm Not to mention all the truck stop food I can afford!! And I can create my favorite Vannila Mountain Dew. But it will be a lot of funn. I've been meaning to watch the movie again for quite some time, no procrastinating now.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:23 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: ..... my favorite Vannila Mountain Dew....

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:26 pm Lol, you just take normal Mountain Dew and use the vanilla shots they put in the machines. It's quite good.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:30 pm I'd have been less disturbed if you said that west-of-the-mountains, they drink raw sheep's blood or something. Mountain Dew? *Mountain Dew*? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=i8YnkTZnutc

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:34 pm Due to dial-up connection speeds of 26.4 kbps. I cannot watch that video right now =P

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:36 pm IrkenEvangelion wrote: Due to dial-up connection speeds of 26.4 kbps. I cannot watch that video right now =P

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:44 pm LOL! That's awesome. BTW Sorry for diverting the topic.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sat Aug 16, 2008 9:48 pm V wrote: I'd have been less disturbed if you said that west-of-the-mountains, they drink raw sheep's blood or something.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Sat Aug 16, 2008 10:37 pm What if Rei's seemingly unfit posture is actually an indication that she is more than just an emotionless character? Unable to effectively express herself in words, the true thoughts that go on inside Rei's mind subtly manifest themselves in other ways - one such way could be her posture. Or maybe she just likes a certain posture. Like how some people like to fold their arms, some like to stick their hands in their pockets. It could be a trait that doesn't really have much to do with personality. I do find this explanation weaker than the above, since Asuka's posture could be considered 'confident.,' thus making certain details like these to be intentionally related. I mean, even Asuka's fiery orange hair (passion) contrasts with Rei's blue hair (calmness), symbolizing their respective personalities.

Because it seems to ME, that as Shinji is the "outwardly nervous one"....HE should be the one who has an "outwardly nervous body posture", and Rei should be the one whose body posture is "limp and emotionless"

 of like, something in the brain that normally deals with fine motor coordination and (what's the word for "internal spacial sense"? I know there's a word for that) are not connected well, so rhythmic tapping or simply "obsessive compulsive fidgeting" is a common autistic thing? Or, is this just a matter of the background artists screwing up? I don't notice it in EVERY shot; there are many shots where Rei IS indeed limp, but there are also MULTIPLE shots where she's "fidgeting"; i.e. the end of episode 12 she's "hanging on herself" this might just be an animation goof ***Of course, the ultimate reason why I am concerned with the body posture of the main characters is not purely knowledge-for-knowledge's sake, but because if/when Live Action Eva is created this would be a good tip for the live-action actors to know in order to give a more accurate method-acting portrayal. Have Faith.

 more than just an emotionless character? Unable to effectively express herself in words, the true thoughts that go on inside Rei's mind subtly manifest themselves in other ways - one such way could be her posture. Or maybe she just likes a certain posture. Like how some people like to fold their arms, some like to stick their hands in their pockets. It could be a trait that doesn't really have much to do with personality. I do find this explanation weaker than the above, since Asuka's posture could be considered 'confident.,' thus making certain details like these to be intentionally related. I mean, even Asuka's fiery orange hair (passion) contrasts with Rei's blue hair (calmness), symbolizing their respective personalities.
